content: A jet of water having a velocity of 20 m/s strikes a series of plates fixed radially on a wheel revolving in the same direction as the jet at 15 m/s. What is the percentage efficiency of the plates? (round off to one decimal place)
explanation:
To determine the efficiency of the plates, we use the following formula :

$$ \begin{aligned} \text{Efficiency}(\eta) &= \frac{\text{Work done per sec}}{\text{Input work power}} \\\\ &= \frac{F_x \cdot u}{\frac{1}{2} \dot{m} \cdot V_1^2} \\\\ &= \frac{\dot{m} \cdot (V_1 - u) \cdot u}{\frac{1}{2} \dot{m} \cdot V_1^2} \\\\ &= \frac{(20 - 15) \cdot 15}{\frac{1}{2} \cdot (20)^2} \\\\ &= 0.375 \text{ or } 37.5\% \end{aligned} $$

Therefore, the correct option is (A) 37.5%.
